source_jobs.title: Director of Software Engineering
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements.

We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values.

We are seeking a visionary Director of Engineering to lead Cubic³’s full Engineering organisation across Software Development and SIM Systems, driving the technical excellence, scalability, and reliability of all our platforms and products.

This role is for a strategic, people‑centric, and technically strong executive who thrives in a fast‑paced environment and can lead complex teams across multiple domains while shaping the future of global connected mobility.



The Role

The Director of Engineering is accountable for Cubic³’s entire engineering output, maturity, and long‑term technology direction. You will lead a multi‑disciplinary organisation including Software Engineering, Platform/SRE, SIM Technology and QA/Automation ensuring that Cubic³ continues to deliver world‑class, highly available, secure, compliant, and innovative solutions.

You will partner closely with Product, Operations, IT, Commercial, and the Executive Leadership Team to define technology strategy, align on business priorities, and drive Cubic³’s roadmap across all customer segments. You will also be a key voice in shaping Cubic³’s platform evolution, SIM innovation, cloud‑native architectures, and the next generation of connected intelligence services.

This is a senior leadership role responsible for organisational scale, engineering effectiveness, and the reliability of platforms used by global automotive OEMs and Tier‑1 partners.



Responsibilities

Executive Leadership & Strategy

 - Define and own Cubic³’s Engineering vision, strategy, and long‑term technology roadmap across software, network, and SIM domains.

 - Lead a high‑performing, multi‑disciplinary engineering organisation with clear structures, succession pipelines, and leadership development.

 - Work with the ELT to set business priorities, investment decisions, and multi‑year strategic plans.

 - Champion engineering excellence, scalability, automation, and performance across all systems.

Technical & Architectural Ownership

 - Oversee architectural direction for Cubic³’s core platforms, cloud services, and data systems.

 - Ensure robust engineering governance across security, compliance, quality, and operational readiness.

 - Drive modernisation initiatives such as cloud‑native transformation, network virtualisation, microservices evolution, and advanced automation.

Delivery, Operations & Reliability

 - Ensure on‑time, high‑quality delivery of major engineering programmes and customer commitments.

 - Own global platform performance, availability, reliability, and scalability in partnership with SRE and Network teams.

 - Oversee SIM lifecycle management, ensuring world‑class resilience and compliance with telecom standards.

 - Establish KPIs and engineering metrics across cycle time, quality, reliability, and operational health.

People Leadership & Culture

 - Foster a culture of innovation, empowerment, accountability, and continuous improvement.

 - Build and grow engineering leadership talent, ensuring strong management capability across all domains.

 - Promote cross‑functional collaboration and transparent communication.

Stakeholder & Customer Engagement

 - Ac

source_jobs.title: Application Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements.

We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values.

The Role

We are looking for an energetic, enthusiastic Application Support Engineer who is committed to their work, and should be comfortable working in a fast paced, highly flexible environment. The role will be responsible for providing excellent technical support and customer service as part of our Level 2 support.



Responsibilities of this role:

 - Coordinate with support and development teams to resolve application and system issues

 - Design and maintain expertise in flow of application process and systems related to company

 - Administer and resolve applications issues, provide updates and perform root cause analysis

 - Supervise all alerts related to application and system procedures and provide services pro-actively

 - Maintain good professional relationships with counterparts of engineering and customer support departments

 - Organize and implement projects ranging from small to medium size and provide assistance to all processes under guidance of Software Engineers

 - Perform root cause assessment and debug all issues on server domain, and availability of applications

 - Coordinate with test department to assist in releases and deployments to various environments and production outputs

 - Provide support and identify all issues and prepare appropriate documentation all issues and solutions.

 - Manage all production system and recommend ways to optimize performance and provide solution to problems and prepare reports for all problems.

 - Identify and provide resolutions ranging in complexity from medium to high.



Essential Experience and Qualifications

 - Knowledge of Microsoft IIS

 - Knowledge of Database technologies Microsoft SQL Server

 - Working Knowledge of IT Hardware and Software

 - Ability to learn and master employer-specific software

 - Complex problem solving

 - Written and verbal communication skills

 - Customer Focused with excellent written and verbal communication and reporting skills

 - Demonstrable ability to learn technical information, processes and applications quickly

 - Excellent attention to detail and the ability to multitask and work effectively within a fast-growing organization

 - Demonstrated ability to be flexible and take a proactive approach to problem solving

Desired Experience and Qualifications

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure (Azure)

 - Experience using tools like Postman to create and save simple and complex HTTP/s requests.

 - A degree level qualification in a technical discipline such as Computer Science or Software Engineering

 - Basic knowledge of programming concepts, preferable C#

 - Experience in building effective relationships with development teams and clients

New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles.



Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusi

source_jobs.title: SVP of Product
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements.

We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values.

Role Overview

Reporting to the Chief Corporate Officer (CCO), the SVP of Product is the primary driver of Cubic’s product portfolio strategy and market-led innovation. This role is responsible for transforming the company’s technology capabilities into commercially successful, customer-centric products that position Cubic as a leader in global connectivity solutions.

The SVP of Product will define the "what" and the "why" of our roadmap, ensuring our platforms scale from 25M to 100M+ assets by delivering autonomous, intelligent, and secure services that meet the specific needs of automotive, agricultural, and transportation customers worldwide.

 

Strategic Mandate

 - Market Vision: Define a bold product vision that anticipates industry shifts in IoT, autonomous driving, and AI-driven services.

 - Commercial Alignment: Partner with the CCO to ensure the product roadmap aligns with corporate growth objectives, revenue targets, and market expansion.

 - Portfolio Strategy: Translate complex industry shifts (autonomous, V2X, embedded AI) into a coherent, prioritized strategic roadmap.

 - Customer Value: Embed AI/ML and advanced analytics into products to unlock new revenue streams and deliver superior customer experiences.

 - Product Evangelism: Represent Cubic as a product thought leader in industry forums, shaping how OEMs perceive the future of connected mobility.

Key Responsibilities

 - Roadmap Ownership: Own the end-to-end product roadmap for connectivity platforms and software-defined vehicle services.

 - Lifecycle Management: Oversee the full product lifecycle from initial concept and proof-of-concept in innovation labs to global commercialization.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with the CTO and technical teams to ensure architectural innovations in cloud and edge computing meet market requirements.

 - Reporting & Governance: Provide regular strategic updates to the CCO and executive team regarding investment priorities and product performance.

 - M&A Support: Conduct product due diligence for potential partnerships and M&A activities to ensure alignment with the existing ecosystem.

 - Financial Stewardship: Manage product-related budgets and optimize pricing and cost structures for global scale.

 - Team Leadership: Build and mentor a high-performing product management organization characterized by curiosity and strategic clarity.

 

Skills & Competencies

 - Strategic Intuition: Ability to anticipate future market trends and translate them into actionable product strategies.

 - Domain Expertise: Deep knowledge of IoT ecosystems, SaaS platforms, and how AI/ML can be productized for the automotive sector.

 - Commercial Acumen: Exceptional ability to link product decisions to business outcomes, ROI, and customer value.

 - Stakeholder Management: Exceptional ability to inspire internal teams and engage global OEM stakeholders.

Experience Profile

 - Senior Leadership: 15+ years in product or technology leadership, with 5+ years at the SVP or C-suite level.

 - Scale: Proven success in managing products that have scaled from tens of millions to hundreds of millions of users or asse

source_jobs.title: SIM Engineer II
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements.

We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values.


THE ROLE

We are seeking an energetic, enthusiastic SIM Engineer II to join the expanding SIM Engineering team, supporting Cubic’s growing customer base and solutions portfolio.

The SIM Engineering team delivers Cubic solutions in the SIM/eSIM Remote SIM Provisioning technology domain, executing state-of-the-art technology projects with global connectivity reach to the automotive, agriculture, and transport industries. We design, develop, and support connectivity solutions leveraging remote SIM provisioning platforms, managing a complex ecosystem of SIM/eSIM technologies, providing SIM/eSIM card logistics and fulfilment, and supporting a global customer base.

You will have the opportunity to work with emerging connectivity technologies including 5G, non-terrestrial networks, and the evolution of eSIM to the latest GSMA specifications. You should enjoy working proactively in a dynamic, highly flexible environment and bring a positive, open, and analytical mindset to the team.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support the design, validation, and maintenance of electrical profiles for SIM and eSIM products

 - Design and implement Java Card applets for SIM/eSIM services, working to 3GPP, ETSI, and GSMA standards

 - Write and maintain technical documentation (e.g. applet specification, electrical profile specifications, APIs, test plans)

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams (mobile core network, security, IoT device vendors, smart card (eUICC) vendors)

 - Develop, test, and support SIM-based applications and tools supporting SIM applications and their integration within the SIM/eSIM ecosystem

 - Contribute to peer review of electrical test data and reports, supporting the technical growth of team members


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

This is an excellent opportunity for a SIM Engineer II to gain hands-on experience of the complete end-to-end process of SIM/eSIM ordering and provisioning, SIM/eSIM Over-the-Air (OTA) infrastructure and management, SIM Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP) solutions for M2M and IoT, SIM–device–network interaction, and more. You’ll work in a small team of experienced engineers at the cutting edge of M2M and IoT eSIM technologies, supporting technical teams across full end-to-end solutions and gaining exposure to diverse technical areas.


REQUIRED SKILLS

 - Third level qualification in a technology discipline such as Engineering, Computer Science, or similar

 - 3–6 years’ experience working in a technology organisation, preferably telecoms technology, with up to 3 years’ experience in SIM/eSIM/OTA engineering R&D

 - Experience with Remote SIM Provisioning technologies (GSMA SGP.02, GSMA SGP.22/SGP.32)

 - Good knowledge of SIM/eSIM architecture and protocols (APDU, ISO 7816)

 - Familiarity with 3GPP/ETSI standards relevant to SIMs (e.g., TS 31.102, TS 102.221)

 - Experience with smart card test & trace tools (e.g., Comprion)

 - Understanding of secure coding practices and cryptographic protocols

 - Proficiency with version control systems (e.g., Git) and development tools

 - Knowledge of SIM operating systems for secure elements (e.g., MULTOS, native SIM OS)

 - Experience working with MNOs or h

source_jobs.title: Senior Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Company

Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements.

We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values.

Role Overview

Responsible for leading the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of security engineering practices across cloud, on-premise, and hybrid environments. This role drives security-by-design principles, DevSecOps integration, Cloud and platform hardening, while providing technical leadership across the organisation.



Key Responsibilities

 - Define and implement security-by-design principles across cloud, on-premise, endpoint, and network environments

 - Embed security into CI/CD pipelines (DevSecOps) in collaboration with engineering and DevOps teams

 - Conduct and lead security risk assessments, ensuring mitigation strategies are implemented across new applications, platforms, and third-party tools

 - Own and enhance cloud, platform, and endpoint security posture, including monitoring for configuration drift and vulnerabilities

 - Develop and enforce hardening standards across endpoints (Windows/macOS), servers, and compute environments

 - Support and partner with teams across IAM, GRC, DLP, SOC, and Vulnerability Management to strengthen overall security capability

 - Own and manage security tools related to the role and discipline e.g. SAST Tooling

 - Drive automation, AI usage and tooling improvements to increase efficiency and reduce risk

 - Provide support to the business as it relates to solution design, project and change management security.

 - Provide technical leadership and incident response support during security events

 - Collaborate with IT, DevOps, Software, Networks and Infra teams to embed security practices

 - Stay current on emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and security technologies



Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Cybersecurity, or related field

 - 8+ years of experience in cybersecurity or information security roles.

 - Experience designing and implementing enterprise scale security principles

 - Proven ability to influence and work with cross-functional teams and departments

 - Hands on experience with end user and cloud security (Azure, AWS, Windows and MAC O/S)

 - Strong technical knowledge of software development (CI/CD pipeline), cloud and security by design ways of working.

 - Experience with security frameworks (NIST, ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2 TISAX)



Key Skills Required

 - Security architecture & Zero Trust principles

 - Cloud security (Azure & AWS)

 - DevSecOps and CI/CD security integration (SDLC)

 - Security frameworks & compliance (ISO 27001, NIST, NIS2, TISAX)

 - Workflow Automation & AI adoption

 - Stakeholder management & technical leadership

Tool Experience

 - Microsoft Azure (E5 Toolset) - Defender for Cloud, Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Identity, Microsoft Purview

 - AWS Security Hub (CSPM), Amazon Guard Duty, AWS Inspector, AWS Identity Analyser, AWS Config

 - SonarCloud (SAST Tooling)

 - Atlassian Cloud (Confluence / Jira)

 - Vanta (GRC)

 - Strong Microsoft Office 365 knowledge

Certifications (Desired)

 - ISC2 - CISSP/CCSP/CSSLP

 - AWS Certified Security - Specialty

 - Microsoft Azure Security Engineer Associate (AZ-500)

 - Microsoft Cybersecurity Architect Expert (SC-100)

notes: Highly technical job postings with emphasis on software engineering and security.
